segments.text: If a man desires the position of a bishop, he desires a good work.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, temperate, sober-minded, of good behavior, hospitable, able to teach;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: one who rules his own house well, having his children in submission with all reverence
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: for if a man does not know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of the church of God?
not a novice, lest being puffed up with pride he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Moreover he must have a good testimony among those who are outside, lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Likewise deacons must be reverent, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not greedy for money,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: holding the mystery of the faith with a pure conscience.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But let these also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons, being found blameless.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Likewise, their wives must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Let deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a good standing and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: These things I write to you,
though I hope to come to you shortly;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: but if I am delayed, I write so that you may know how you ought to conduct yourself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifested in the flesh, Justified in the Spirit, Seen by angels, Preached among the Gentiles, Believed on in the world, Received up in glory.
segments.speaker: Paul
